content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 2.2 WARN — release_notes.txt calls this a "public beta" and warns of "rough edges" (fastlane/metadata/en-US/release_notes.txt:1-2), implying an unfinished build.
Pierre: Apple reviewers read release notes as store-facing copy, and language like "beta", "still testing", and "expect rough edges" suggests the build isn't ready for production, which can trigger a rejection or review question under 2.2. The description.txt looks fine, but the release notes need rewriting to describe the update professionally without beta/testing disclaimers. Replace with something like "This update improves sync reliability" before submission.
